expectations.text: signup-form-hook: signup form 상태를 담당하는 훅이 hooks/utils/signup 경로에 생성된다 (예: hooks/utils/signup/useSignupForm/index.ts)
signup-api-hook: signup submit을 담당하는 훅이 hooks/apis/auth/mutations/usePostSignup 경로에 생성된다
expectations.evidence: No file at hooks/apis/auth/mutations/usePostSignup exists. Only two files were produced: the form hook and page.tsx. The signup/auth logic is handled directly inside useSignupForm via useAuth context.

expectations.text: hook-use-prefix: 훅 이름이 use 접두사로 시작한다
expectations.evidence: The hook is named 'useSignupForm' (const useSignupForm = ...) — starts with 'use' prefix.

expectations.text: hook-index-ts: 비 API 훅이 hooks/utils/{domain}/{hookName}/index.ts 패턴으로 생성된다
expectations.evidence: File is at hooks/utils/signup/useSignupForm/index.ts, matching the hooks/utils/{domain}/{hookName}/index.ts pattern.

expectations.text: page-no-direct-logic: page에서 직접적인 fetch/business logic이 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: app/signup/page.tsx only imports useSignupForm and destructures its return values. No fetch calls, no useState, no business logic remains in the page component.

expectations.text: confirm-password-preserved: confirm password validation 흐름이 보존된다
expectations.evidence: validateField checks 'confirmPassword' field: value !== fields.password returns '비밀번호가 일치하지 않습니다'. The validate() function also checks password !== confirmPassword. The page renders the confirmPassword input with handleChange/handleBlur and shows errors.confirmPassword.
Hook is defined as 'const useSignupForm = (): UseSignupFormReturn => { ... }' — arrow function syntax.

expectations.text: handle-prefix: 이벤트 핸들러가 handle 접두사를 사용한다
expectations.evidence: All event handlers use the handle prefix: handleChange, handleBlur, handleSubmit as seen in both the hook definition and the page usage.
